name: Connection Timeout
description: TCP connection to the target host times out before establishing a handshake. Adapter receives no response.
mitigation: Implement retry with exponential backoff; log timeout event; fail after max retries.

name: DNS Resolution Failure
description: The hostname of the target server cannot be resolved to an IP address.
mitigation: Validate hostname configuration; implement fallback DNS servers; fail with clear error.

name: TLS Certificate Expired
description: The server presents an expired or otherwise invalid TLS certificate.
mitigation: Check certificate validity; log expiration date; fail securely (no insecure fallback).

name: Malformed MOS XML Payload
description: The MOS adapter receives or generates XML that is not well-formed or does not conform to the MOS DTD.
mitigation: Validate XML against schema before sending; catch parse errors with descriptive messages.

name: Unexpected MOS Response Status
description: MOS adapter receives a response status that does not match any known status (roAck, roNack, roReq, roList).
mitigation: Validate status against known enum; log unexpected status; treat as roNack for safety.

name: REST Method Not Allowed
description: HTTP request uses a method (e.g., PATCH) that is not in the allowed set for the given endpoint.
mitigation: Validate method against endpoint schema before dispatching; return structured 405 error.

name: Missing Required Field in Payload
description: A required JSON or XML field is missing from the request or response payload.
mitigation: Validate payload against schema; flag missing field; return validation error.

name: Type Mismatch in Argument Value
description: A command argument value has the wrong type (e.g., string where number expected).
mitigation: Coerce types where safe; fail validation where coercion is ambiguous.

name: Data Truncation
description: A payload field exceeds the maximum allowed length and is silently truncated or rejected.
mitigation: Enforce maxLength constraints; log truncation warnings; prevent silent data loss.

name: Command Execution Timeout
description: A *OD or *CG command takes longer than the configured timeout to produce a result.
mitigation: Set command-level timeout; cancel on timeout; log partial results if available.

name: Idle Connection Closed
description: The server closes the connection due to inactivity before the client sends a request.
mitigation: Implement keep-alive; reconnect transparently with state recovery.

name: Stale Data Detected
description: The data retrieved from a source has a timestamp older than the configured freshness threshold.
mitigation: Check timestamp freshness; trigger data refresh; log staleness warning.
